description:
You are a DevOps engineer who has been asked to triage a small application-log
    file and to prepare a short report for the on-call run-book.

    Environment
    ===========
    • Your working directory is /home/user.  
    • A directory called /home/user/pipeline already exists.  
    • Inside that directory there is a single log file called
      /home/user/pipeline/app.log.

    Goal
    ====
    1. Read the existing /home/user/pipeline/app.log file.  
    2. Produce a new file named /home/user/pipeline/error_summary.log.  
       The automated tests will read **only** this file to judge correctness,  
       so make sure it is created with the exact format described below.  
    3. Use no more than 3-4 standard GNU/Linux commands
       (think along the lines of grep, awk, sort, uniq, head, tail, etc.)
       to do the work; you do NOT have to show the commands in your reply,
       just run them in the shell.

    Required content of error_summary.log
    -------------------------------------
    • The file must be plain UTF-8 text, with Unix line endings.  
    • The first three lines are a table of unique error codes and how many
      times each error appears in app.log, sorted from the most common to
      the least common.  
        Format (no header line):
            <ERROR_CODE><TAB><COUNT>
      Example:
            ERR1001<TAB>2  
            ERR1002<TAB>1  
            ERR1003<TAB>1  

      – “<TAB>” means a real tab character (ASCII 0x09).  
      – The automated tests will check for the exact order and for the tab.

    • After the summary table, insert a single line containing exactly:
            ---recent-errors---

    • After that marker line, append up to the last five ERROR lines
      (chronological order as they appear in the log).  
      If the log contains fewer than five ERROR lines, list them all.
      The lines must be copied verbatim from app.log.

      Example:
            [2023-11-10 10:01:07] ERROR [ERR1003] Null pointer exception  
            [2023-11-10 10:01:06] ERROR [ERR1001] Failed to connect to DB  
            [2023-11-10 10:01:05] ERROR [ERR1002] Timeout in service  
            [2023-11-10 10:01:03] ERROR [ERR1001] Failed to connect to DB  

    • No blank lines are allowed anywhere in error_summary.log.

    Error-handling requirement
    --------------------------
    If the log contains **zero** ERROR lines, error_summary.log must contain
    exactly ONE line with the literal text:

        NO_ERRORS_FOUND

    (That special case is also verified by automated tests.)

    Deliverables
    ============
    • /home/user/pipeline/error_summary.log in the precise format above.  
    • After creating the file, output its contents to the console (e.g. using
      “cat”), so the result is visible in the run transcript.

    Constraints
    ===========
    • Do not move or rename the existing app.log.  
    • Do not create any additional files besides error_summary.log.  
    • You do not have root privileges, so stay inside /home/user.

    Tips
    ====
    – Think “grep ERROR”, “awk” to pull the code between “[” and “]”,  
      “sort | uniq -c” for counts, and “tail -n 5” for the recent section.  
    – You can build the file in a temporary location and then mv it into place
      if that helps keep the command chain short.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts
    -------------------------------------
    Directory:
        /home/user/pipeline      (rwx for user)

    File:
        /home/user/pipeline/app.log
        ----------------------------------------------------------------
        [2023-11-10 10:01:02] INFO  Starting job
        [2023-11-10 10:01:03] ERROR [ERR1001] Failed to connect to DB
        [2023-11-10 10:01:04] WARN  Slow response
        [2023-11-10 10:01:05] ERROR [ERR1002] Timeout in service
        [2023-11-10 10:01:06] ERROR [ERR1001] Failed to connect to DB
        [2023-11-10 10:01:07] ERROR [ERR1003] Null pointer exception
        [2023-11-10 10:01:08] INFO  Job completed
        ----------------------------------------------------------------

    Expected final state after a correct solution
    ---------------------------------------------
    File:
        /home/user/pipeline/error_summary.log
        ----------------------------------------------------------------
        ERR1001<TAB>2
        ERR1002<TAB>1
        ERR1003<TAB>1
        ---recent-errors---
        [2023-11-10 10:01:07] ERROR [ERR1003] Null pointer exception
        [2023-11-10 10:01:06] ERROR [ERR1001] Failed to connect to DB
        [2023-11-10 10:01:05] ERROR [ERR1002] Timeout in service
        [2023-11-10 10:01:03] ERROR [ERR1001] Failed to connect to DB
        ----------------------------------------------------------------
        (There is a literal tab between each ERROR_CODE and COUNT on the
         first three lines; no blank line at the end of the file.)

    The automated test suite will:
    1. Assert that /home/user/pipeline/error_summary.log exists.
    2. Read the file and compare it byte-for-byte with the block above.
       If “NO_ERRORS_FOUND” is present instead, it will treat that as the
       zero-error edge case.
